original_abstract: Written, spoken, and arbitrary, Arabic has a distinct language style. In the same way, there are many domains for Arabic knowledge. You'll be exposed to a wide range of subjects when studying Arabic. Morphology, often known as shorof science, is one genre. In terms of morphology, the goal of this study is to characterise the linguistic features of surah An-Naba' in the Qur'an. Library research is the method used in this kind of study. This research seeks to determine the number of fi'il mudhori in Surah An-naba juz 30 and their reading pattern, in addition to identifying the dhomir that appears in each fi'il mudhori sentence. And describes in terms of the nahwu and shorof science approaches. The research results illustrate that researchers found 16 words that included fiil mudhari, these 16 words had been included in several different wazans and dhomir-dhomir.
humanized_abstract: Arabic as written, spoken or adjectives language has its own characteristics. Like that, there are many fields of knowledge of Arabic. For every field of knowledge of Arabic, many subjects will be studied. In the field of Arabic morphology that is also called shorof science in universities, this study aimed at identifying linguistic features found in surah An-Naba' in the Holy Qur'an. The library research method is used in this study. The objective of this study is to determine the number of words of fi’il mudhori that exist in Surah An-naba (juz 30) and how these words are read as well as to identify the dhomir that exist in every sentence of the word of fi’il mudhori and described it by using methods of nahwu and shorof science. And the conclusion reached from the result of this study shows that the researcher found out that there are 16 words in surah An-Naba' that consists of fi'il mudhari. These 16 words are scattered on various patterns of wazans and dhomir-dhomir.
